methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Volund Manufacturing is a seed-stage defense and security company founded by Eric Hostetler. Valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 5 same-sector and adjacent-stage comparables spanning seed and Series A, supplemented by a broader universe of 8 private and 2 public defense-tech companies including Anduril Industries, Shield AI, Saronic, and Flock Safety.

The peer-median method applied across the 5 comparable companies yields an implied valuation of $150M. The peer set ranges from Tenna Systems at $60M (seed stage) to Stark Defence at $500M (Series A), with intermediate points at NODA AI ($125M), Ares Industries ($150M), and Vatn Systems ($300M). This wide dispersion reflects stage and capability variance within the defense sector. The median of $150M anchors the analysis given the coherence of stage and sector alignment within the peer group.

Triangulation across available methods supports a valuation range of $128M to $172M, with a median point of $150M. The single-method limitation warrants caution: revenue multiples, comparable transaction data, or DCF analysis would strengthen conviction if available. Key uncertainties include the company's revenue traction, customer concentration, and differentiation relative to the private peer set, which are not quantified in available data. Seed-stage companies in this sector typically show high valuation volatility pending customer validation and capital efficiency metrics.
